title: Expiratory Assistance (Lateral Chest Squeeze)
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Patients experiencing moderate to severe respiratory distress with prolonged expiratory phase and gas trapping, such as asthmatic and COPD patients.

#### Procedure

- Explain the procedure to the patient, if conscious.

- If the patient is sitting, sit in front of patient and place both hands on either side of the lower rib cage.

- If the patient is supine, kneel astride hips and place both hands on either side of the lower rib cage.

- As the patient ceases inspiration (either spontaneously or with manual ventilation), compress ribs medially and in a synchronistic and slow manner to accommodate the entire expiratory phase.

- Do not compress the patient's ribs during inspiration.

- Compressions should be slow, rhythmic and sustained to assist in forcing trapped air out of the lungs.

#### Contraindications

- Nil when indicated.

#### Complications

- Hypotension.
- Fractured ribs.
